title: Socially efficient and inefficient market outcomes
description: Practice what you've learned about marginal social cost and marginal social benefit in this exercise.
topic_path: Market failure and the role of government
Socially efficient and inefficient market outcomes
Socially efficient and inefficient market outcomes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the market quantity that will always maximize total economic surplus?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The quantity where the marginal benefit of the last unit equals the marginal cost of producing the last unit.
The quantity where supply equals demand.
The quantity where the value of consuming one more of a good is higher than the cost of producing one more good.
The quantity where the value of consuming one more of a good is less than the cost of producing one more good.
The quantity where the cost per unit is minimized.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following must be true to maximize economic surplus in a market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All social costs and benefits are internalized by agents in the market.
Marginal private cost equals marginal private benefit.
All goods must be common resources.
All goods must be public goods.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes when an agent manipulates an environment to achieve market power that serves no productive purpose?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**When a rational agent is making a decision that is individually rational and individually optimal, what do they do?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: equate private marginal benefit to private marginal cost
equate social marginal benefit to social marginal cost
equate marginal social benefit to marginal private cost
equate private marginal benefit to social marginal cost
choose an outcome where private marginal benefit exceeds the private marginal cost

assessment_items.question_content:
The Ministry of Economics has determined that the production of firebolts creates pollution that costs society $\$120$ per firebolt to clean up. The private benefit of producing firebolts is $\$80$, and each firebolt can be purchased for $\$80$. Currently, $100$ firebolts are being produced. 

**If these policymakers want to eliminate the market inefficiency created by the production of firebolts, which of the following should they do?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: use cost-benefit analysis to evaluate different policies

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of the following are associated with an inefficient allocation of resources EXCEPT**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**All of the following result in an efficient allocation of resources EXCEPT**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: perfect competition in the long run
public goods produced in sufficient quantity
private costs and benefits reflect all costs and benefits

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of the following are associated with a market quantity that is the quantity where social marginal cost (MSC) doesn't equal marginal social benefit (MSB) EXCEPT?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
